title: C# SOLID — Paper 2
description: Covers SRP refactoring, OCP concepts, OCP refactoring, LSP concepts, LSP refactoring, Strategy Pattern, Factory & Abstract Factory, Decorator Pattern, Observer Pattern, and Repository Pattern.

questions.text: ST06-1: When fixing an LSP violation, which of the following is the correct approach to postconditions in a subclass?

options.text: The subclass may weaken postconditions to give callers more flexibility.
The subclass must strengthen or maintain postconditions to ensure promises made by the base class are kept.
Postconditions in subclasses are optional and can be ignored.
The subclass may remove postconditions if the new logic is simpler.

questions.text: ST15-4: In the Observer pattern, what is the term for an object that wants to be notified of changes?

options.text: Publisher
Subject
Observer (subscriber)
Mediator

questions.text: ST02-4: Which refactoring technique is most appropriate when a method in a class is primarily concerned with data from another class?

options.text: Extract Class
Move Method
Inline Method
Introduce Parameter Object

questions.text: ST13-4: In C#, the Factory Method pattern is commonly implemented using:

options.text: A static class with a single Create() method.
An abstract base class declaring a factory method that subclasses override to produce specific products.
A sealed class that uses reflection to instantiate types.
An enum-based switch to choose a concrete class.

questions.text: ST03-4: Which of the following code structures best demonstrates adherence to OCP?

options.text: A single method with switch-case statements for each supported format.
A set of classes each implementing an IExporter interface, registered via a factory.
A God class that handles all export logic internally.
A static utility class with overloaded export methods.

questions.text: ST16-1: What is the primary goal of the Repository pattern?

options.text: To cache database query results in memory.
To abstract the data access layer behind an interface, decoupling domain logic from persistence concerns.
To enforce referential integrity in the database.
To provide a direct connection to a specific ORM framework.

questions.text: ST12-4: Which of the following best describes the 'context' class in the Strategy pattern?

options.text: The abstract interface that all strategies must implement.
The class that holds a reference to a strategy and delegates algorithm execution to it.
The class that decides which strategy to choose at compile time.
A concrete algorithm implementation.

questions.text: ST05-4: A `FlyingBird` base class has a `Fly()` method. A `Penguin` subclass inherits from it and throws `NotSupportedException` in `Fly()`. This is a violation of:

options.text: The Single Responsibility Principle
The Open/Closed Principle
The Liskov Substitution Principle
The Interface Segregation Principle

questions.text: ST04-4: In C#, which of the following patterns allows adding new tax rules to a tax engine without modifying existing tax classes?

options.text: Modify the existing TaxEngine.Calculate() method each time.
Define an ITaxRule interface and add new ITaxRule implementations, composing them in the engine.
Add a new public property to the TaxEngine class for each new rule.
Derive a new TaxEngine subclass for every new tax rule.

questions.text: ST14-4: Which of the following best illustrates using the Decorator pattern in C# for logging?

options.text: Adding a log statement inside every existing service method.
Creating a LoggingOrderService class that implements IOrderService, wraps a real OrderService, and logs before/after each call.
Inheriting from OrderService and overriding every method.
Using a static Logger.Log() call inside the OrderService class.

questions.text: ST02-5: In SRP refactoring, 'Extract Class' is used when:

options.text: A class is too small and should merge with another.
Part of a class's behaviour is cohesive enough to stand on its own as a separate class.
A method is too long and should be split into overloads.
An interface has too many methods.

questions.text: ST06-2: A `Rectangle` has virtual properties `Width` and `Height`. A `Square` subclass overrides both setters so that setting Width also sets Height. What LSP fix is most appropriate?

options.text: Make Rectangle a sealed class.
Remove the Width and Height properties from Rectangle.
Remove the inheritance relationship and treat Square and Rectangle as separate classes sharing an IShape interface.
Add a flag property IsSquare to Rectangle.

questions.text: ST05-5: According to LSP, which of the following is a valid behaviour for a subclass method?

options.text: Throwing a new exception type not documented by the base class method.
Accepting a wider range of input values than the base class method.
Returning a narrower range of output values than the base class method.
Requiring more conditions to be satisfied before it can be called.

questions.text: ST03-5: A developer must add a new payment provider to a checkout system. The system currently has a `ProcessPayment(string providerName)` method with a switch statement. What is the OCP-compliant fix?

options.text: Add the new case to the existing switch statement.
Refactor to use an IPaymentProvider interface and register the new provider without touching existing code.
Create a new ProcessPaymentV2 method alongside the old one.
Use reflection to discover and call the new provider class automatically.

questions.text: ST16-2: How does the Repository pattern support the Dependency Inversion Principle?

options.text: By coupling domain services directly to Entity Framework DbContext.
By defining an IRepository interface that domain services depend on, allowing the actual storage mechanism to be swapped.
By storing domain logic inside repository methods.
By making all repository methods static.

questions.text: ST12-5: In a C# Strategy pattern, replacing a large if-else block with strategies relates most directly to which refactoring technique?

options.text: Extract Method
Introduce Null Object
Replace Conditional with Polymorphism
Collapse Hierarchy

questions.text: ST04-5: Which keyword in C# prevents a class from being further subclassed, effectively closing it for extension via inheritance?

options.text: abstract
virtual
sealed
readonly

questions.text: ST13-5: An Abstract Factory is used in a cross-platform UI toolkit. When adding support for a new OS, what change is needed?

options.text: Modify every existing factory class to add OS-specific conditions.
Create a new concrete factory class implementing the abstract factory interface for the new OS.
Add a new OS parameter to every existing product creation method.
Change the interface to include the new OS type as a concrete method.

questions.text: ST06-3: Which of the following best describes an LSP-compliant inheritance hierarchy?

options.text: Every subclass adds at least five new methods not present in the base class.
Subclasses can be used wherever the base class is expected, without callers needing to know the difference.
Subclasses may throw new unchecked exceptions from any overridden method.
Subclasses always use `new` (hiding) rather than `override`.

questions.text: ST15-5: Which C# feature allows subscribing to an event without being able to raise it or unsubscribe other handlers?

options.text: A public delegate field.
The event keyword with += subscription syntax.
A public Action property.
A static event declared in an interface.

questions.text: ST02-6: When should you apply the 'Separate concerns into services' SRP refactoring?

options.text: When a class has too many private helper methods.
When a class mixes domain logic with infrastructure concerns such as database calls or file I/O.
When a class has only one public method.
When two classes share the same base class.

questions.text: ST16-3: When unit testing with a Repository pattern, which test double is typically used in place of the real repository?

options.text: A spy that records database calls.
An in-memory fake or mock implementing the IRepository interface.
The real database with a test schema.
A stub that always returns null.

questions.text: ST03-6: Bertrand Meyer's original formulation of OCP described classes as:

options.text: Open for modification once per release cycle.
Open (for extension) and closed (for modification) simultaneously.
Closed for extension until all tests pass.
Open only to internal modifications by the owning team.

questions.text: ST04-6: Which design level OCP approach does Robert C. Martin associate with polymorphism in object-oriented languages?

options.text: Using global configuration flags to enable or disable features.
Closing modules against change through abstract interfaces that clients depend on.
Recompiling the entire application for each new feature.
Using compile-time generics exclusively for extension points.

questions.text: ST05-6: In C#, which of the following inheritance scenarios does NOT violate LSP?

options.text: A subclass that overrides a method and always throws NotImplementedException.
A subclass that overrides a method but returns a more specific (covariant) type that still fulfils the base contract.
A subclass that ignores the base class method and uses a completely different algorithm with different observable side effects.
A subclass that strengthens a precondition by requiring a non-null parameter when the base accepts null.

questions.text: ST12-6: A shipping cost calculator uses if-else chains to handle Standard, Express, and Overnight shipping. Applying the Strategy pattern, what represents a 'strategy' here?

options.text: The ShippingCostCalculator class itself.
Each shipping mode (Standard, Express, Overnight) implemented as a separate class behind an IShippingStrategy interface.
The if-else chain refactored into a switch expression.
A dictionary mapping shipping type strings to cost multipliers.

questions.text: ST13-6: In a game engine, a MonsterFactory creates different monster types. Each monster type requires different weapons and armor created together. Which pattern best supports this?

options.text: Factory Method — one factory per monster.
Abstract Factory — one factory interface creating related weapon+armor pairs per monster type.
Singleton — a single global factory for all monsters.
Builder — constructing each monster step by step.

questions.text: ST06-4: An overridden method in a subclass silently does nothing (empty body) where the base class performs an important operation. This violates LSP because:

options.text: The subclass adds too many new methods.
The subclass weakens the postcondition, breaking the behaviour contract expected by callers of the base type.
The subclass does not have enough methods.
The empty method speeds up execution, which is always desirable.

questions.text: ST14-5: The Decorator pattern uses composition rather than inheritance. What is the primary benefit of this choice?

options.text: It reduces the number of interfaces needed in the system.
Decorators can be stacked flexibly at runtime without creating an explosion of subclass combinations.
Composition is faster than inheritance at runtime.
It ensures the decorated object is always a singleton.

questions.text: ST15-6: IObservable<T> and IObserver<T> in .NET are part of which library and represent which pattern?

options.text: LINQ; Query pattern
Reactive Extensions (Rx.NET); Observer/Push-based pattern
TPL Dataflow; Pipeline pattern
ASP.NET Core; Middleware pattern
